pattern: Compute the decoded length by scanning forward, then walk the encoded string backward, repeatedly reducing the target index modulo the current length so digit-repeats are unwound without ever materializing the expanded tape.
hints: The decoded string can be astronomically long, so building it is hopeless; instead reason about lengths and which original character a position maps to.
First find the total decoded length by multiplying on each digit and adding one on each letter; then process characters from the end.
Going backward, take the target index modulo the current length on each step: a digit divides the length back down, and when you land on a letter at a zero remainder that letter is the answer.

edge_inputs.description: nested repeats, index inside a later copy
two trailing digits compound the length
enormous length but the first letter is asked
first character of a simple repeat
index points at the very last decoded letter

stress.description: short encodings with a modest k so the capped brute build stays cheap
stress.note: decoded length kept bounded so the oracle can build up to k characters
